DetectionRule.Name: Phishing Link Click and Credential Harvesting
DetectionRule.Description: Detects users clicking phishing links that lead to credential harvesting pages or malicious downloads. Analyzes URL clicks, browser navigation patterns, and subsequent authentication attempts to identify phishing campaigns.
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT41, FIN7, TA505, Phishing Groups

RemediationSteps.Step: Warn affected user about potential phishing attack
Reset passwords for the compromised account
Enable MFA if not already enabled
Block the malicious URL/domain at DNS and web proxy
Scan device for malware or credential stealers
Conduct security awareness training for affected user

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude security team testing and authorized scans
Time-based: Focus on unusual navigation patterns
User-based: Exclude users with security roles

DetectionRule.Name: Malicious Attachment Execution
DetectionRule.Description: Detects execution of files from email attachments or downloads that exhibit malicious characteristics. Identifies suspicious file types, macro execution, and script-based attacks commonly delivered through phishing emails.

RemediationSteps.Step: Scan device for additional malware infections
Check for lateral movement from the compromised device
Reset passwords for affected user accounts
Enable Enhanced Office Macro Security policies

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized macro-enabled documents from trusted sources
Time-based: Alert on unusual execution times
Location-based: Focus on Downloads/Temp folder executions

DetectionRule.Name: External Remote Service Compromise
DetectionRule.Description: Detects initial access through compromised external-facing services including VPN, Citrix, RDP, and other remote access solutions. Identifies anomalous logins, brute force attempts, and exploitation of vulnerable remote services.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1190 - Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1078 - Valid Accounts
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT28, FIN7, Ransomware Groups, APT41

RemediationSteps.Step: Block source IP addresses at firewall
Force password reset and MFA re-enrollment
Review VPN/RDP logs for unauthorized access
Check for lateral movement from entry point
Implement additional security controls (MFA, IP restrictions)

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude known VPN concentrator IP addresses
Exclude legitimate remote workers during travel
Time-based: Allow maintenance window access attempts
User-based: Exclude IT admin accounts with special access needs
